"'''It Takes a Thief'''" is a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story from ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 6]]''. It features [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] and [[Na-Jia]].

==Plot summary==
[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] sits in a bar on the snow planet [[Diado]], taunted by the local gang leader [[Birok]] for being a newcomer to town. Showing his strength by publicly humiliating Birok, Tiin is pulled aside by a girl named [[Na-Jia]], who promptly steals his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Demanding his weapon back, Tiin provides the girl with 600 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] and then makes his way down the street, where he is surrounded by [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]] almost immediately. Although he fights valiantly, a droid still manages to sneak up behind the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. He is saved when Na-Jia unexpectedly returns, shooting the droid in the back. Tiin is ensnared by a rope flung from the back of Birok's [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] and dragged around town. The gang leader, not considering how useless a rope is when compared to a lightsaber, is awestruck as his "captive" escapes and defeats Birok's men. More droid reinforcements arrive, however, and Tiin is captured once more, along with Na-Jia. The Separatist officers, believing Na-Jia to be a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]], take her in for questioning. Na-Jia, pretending to have a fainting spell, falls on top of an officer holding Tiin's lightsaber, and manages to snatch it. With the help of a weapon, the two are able to escape and steal a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], which Tiin uses to distract the Separatists while Na-Jia escapes. Blowing apart the balcony where Birok and his Separatist friends are watching the goings-on, Tiin turns his guns upon the station's power core. His mission completed, the Jedi returns home, smiling as a purple-haired figure waves to him from the snow.
